overview: The Village of Weston Board of Trustees held its first meeting with three newly elected trustees, approving liquor license renewals, preliminary assessment resolutions for three street reconstruction projects, and a solid waste ordinance amendment. The board debated remote meeting attendance policy, ultimately voting 6-1 to allow remote attendance with camera requirements while prohibiting remote participation in closed sessions. Significant discussion occurred regarding Kennedy Park renovation funding transparency.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Acknowledge reports from boards, committees, and commissions
Department reports (Administrator, Clerk, Finance, EMS/Fire, Parks & Rec, Planning & Development, Police, Public Works, Technology)
Consent agenda with liquor license renewals
Preliminary assessment resolutions for street reconstructions
Kennedy Park renovation quarterly update discussion
President's appointments to committees and commissions

discussions.body: Joe Jordan (4102 Camp Phillips Road) gave extended remarks welcoming new trustees and praising village staff, noting the village 'is not broke' and 'management isn't broken.' He reflected on his service, the fire department staffing needs, and defended the decision not to hold a referendum on the village hall building. Jim Pinsel criticized President Maloney's appointment of Louise Lopez as vice president despite Lopez not receiving any votes in the election, and objected to former trustees being reappointed to committees, calling it 'lack of transparency and disregard for the will of the people.'

The board identified corrections needed to the March 16, 2026 minutes, including that the meeting actually occurred on March 23rd due to a snowstorm postponement, and that Trustee Cronin was marked excused but listed as calling the meeting to order. Motion by Lopez Saro, second by Clark to approve with corrections. Passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Consent Agenda and Liquor License Renewals
discussions.body: Item 41 was pulled from consent agenda. Trustee Barb Oling made motion to approve liquor license renewals with a correction to Fairfield Inn and Suites' premise description to include only 'lobby, locked fridges, and locked storage room along with the south end hallway' rather than the entire hotel. Second by Olsen. Passed unanimously.
Motion to approve by unidentified trustee, second by Zagami. Passed unanimously with no discussion.

discussions.item: Preliminary Assessment Resolutions for Street Reconstructions
discussions.body: Three resolutions passed for Gelick/Alderson, Bladel Avenue, and Concord Avenue/Bay's Street reconstructions. Director Michael explained assessments only cover driveway approaches (approximately $1,000-$1,500 per property) payable over 10 years. All three resolutions passed unanimously.

Extensive discussion occurred with trustees seeking clarity on funding sources. The Friends of Kennedy Park foundation account has $84,000, not including glow tournament proceeds. Trustee Beck and Trustee Zagami pressed for more transparency on fundraising efforts and grant applications. Director Stankevich explained the $851,000 in capital funds included unspent park project allocations over 20 years and budget surplus funds. Trustees expressed concern about public confusion regarding funding, with Zagami noting Kennedy Park was 'one of the biggest hot button issues' during the election. Staff agreed to provide more detailed information at the next meeting.

Trustee Lopez Saro moved to restrict virtual attendance to medical reasons only, requiring notification to clerk/administrator/chair, with camera requirements and prohibition on closed session remote attendance. Second by Zagami. Failed 3-4 (Lopez Saro, Zagami, Olsen in favor; Hushen, Beck, Maloney, Clark opposed). A second motion by Zagami with similar restrictions also failed. President Maloney then moved to allow remote attendance on camera with proper notification, prohibiting closed session remote attendance. Second by Olsen. Passed 6-1 with one opposed (Lopez Saro).

President Maloney presented committee appointments, noting Mark Kern remains on Community Development Authority, Joe Jordan was placed on Planning Commission and Public Works, and Steve Cronin was returned to Planning Commission replacing Travis Hoffman. New trustees were assigned: Katrina Clark to Mountain Bay Metro, Lisa Beck to SAFER, Barb Oling and Hushen Zagami to Everest Metro Joint Finance. Discussion was ongoing at transcript end.

publicComment: Two speakers offered public comment. Joe Jordan (4102 Camp Phillips Road) spoke at length welcoming new trustees, praising staff, and defending village decisions including the building construction and fire department funding approaches. Jim Pinsel criticized President Maloney's appointment of Louise Lopez as vice president and the reappointment of former trustees to committees, calling it favoritism and stating 'the residents overwhelmingly rejected the current policies and directions of the former board members.'
actionItems: Approved March 23, 2026 meeting minutes with date and attendance corrections
Approved liquor license renewals for 2026-2027 with corrected premise description for Fairfield Inn and Suites
Approved Ordinance 26-008 amending Chapter 66 solid waste
Approved Resolution 26-010 preliminary assessment for Gelick and Alderson reconstruction
Approved Resolution 26-011 preliminary assessment for Bladel Avenue reconstruction
Approved Resolution 26-012 preliminary assessment for Concord Avenue and Bay's Street reconstruction
Approved remote meeting attendance policy allowing virtual attendance on camera with notification to clerk, administrator, and chair; closed sessions require in-person attendance
Staff to provide detailed Kennedy Park funding timeline and Friends of Kennedy Park grant application history at next meeting
Clerk to research whether open records requestors should be listed by name in reports
Chief Hunt to forward SWAT analysis for new building to full board
title: Board of Trustees - 4/21/2026
